location: Guaranteed drop from the Misbegotten Warrior + Crucible Knight duo boss fight in Redmane Castle Plaza, Caelid, entered from the Chamber Outside the Plaza Site of Grace. Only accessible while the Radahn Festival is inactive.
tactics: Rest at Chamber Outside the Plaza, then head right through the fog wall into the plaza to start the fight — this is only open before the Radahn Festival begins, or after beating Radahn and exhausting Witch-Hunter Jerren's dialogue.
The fight starts as Misbegotten Warrior alone; the Crucible Knight joins once the Warrior drops to roughly half health, so rush the Warrior down first.
Once split apart, take on the Crucible Knight one-on-one — both bosses telegraph their swings clearly, so wait for openings rather than trading.
Don't confuse this fog wall with the nearby lift down to Wailing Dunes for the Radahn fight itself — they're accessed from the same grace area but lead to different encounters.
lore: Hewn from a chunk of rubble that fell from the sky when a great ruin collapsed, this fragment was later shaped into a weapon that still carries a trace of the destructive force behind that fall.

builds.name: Ultra-Heavy Strength Colossal
builds.why: Strength scaling dwarfs every other stat here (reaching S-tier at +10), and its huge base attack power makes it one of the hardest single swings available to a pure-Strength build.
